column2: # --- Parameters: adjust these cutpoints to see different mappings ---
cutpoints <- c(-3.77, -1.7, 0.2, 2.02)   # four thresholds -> five categories
region_id <- cut(df$x, breaks = breaks, labels = FALSE, right = FALSE)
# Compute implied probabilities (area under logistic CDF)
# if any regions empty because of coarse grid, fallback to cutpoint centers:
p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = dens, fill = region)) +
geom_area(data = subset(df, region == 1), aes(x = x, y = dens), inherit.aes = TRUE) +
geom_area(data = subset(df, region == 2), aes(x = x, y = dens), inherit.aes = TRUE) +
geom_area(data = subset(df, region == 3), aes(x = x, y = dens), inherit.aes = TRUE) +
geom_area(data = subset(df, region == 4), aes(x = x, y = dens), inherit.aes = TRUE) +
geom_area(data = subset(df, region == 5), aes(x = x, y = dens), inherit.aes = TRUE) +
title = "Latent Variable Interpretation — Ordered Outcome (5 levels)",
# add category labels centered in each shaded region
aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
